generatedSentence: Elara, a veteran alchemist with graying temples, felt a strange feeling of unease, though her life had been largely cool since leaving the warfare behind. She spent her days in the foundry, meticulously crafting bronze and golden artifacts. Her only real obligation now was to herself, an inner peace she worked hard to uplift. Sometimes, a visceral memory would tickle at the edges of her mind, a reminder of past journeys and the diverse faces she'd met. She was always thankful for the present, despite the gloom that occasionally threatened to unfold. Her home, a rustic cabin, was on the frontier, far from the mass of humanity. A familiar scent of hickory smoke clung to her vest, a comfort. She’d made many wishes in her youth, but now, simple contentment was her greatest nuggets. Today, she planned to decorate her small study, a quiet right of passage into a new, calmer phase of life. Suddenly, a sudden tremor shook her cabin, more than just the usual thunders. Elara, always preoccupied with strange occurrences, quickly entered a state of heightened alert. She knew these lands harbored killers – not just the human lowlife variety, but something more enigmatic. Old stories of aliens and an antichrist figure, dismissed as indie mythology, now seemed chillingly plausible. Rumors of an undersized yet deadly porcupine-like creature, twisted by gangrene and fueled by impious intent, had circulated among the veterans. A broken engine part, a strange item resembling a launcher, had been found near the perimeter of her isolated property just yesterday. Her aging bicycle lay nearby, its gaskets scratched and a tire broken, as if a hellhound had taken a bite. It was an underwhelming clue, though, for the scale of the impending doom. This wasn't some common tickler; this was something designed for physical action. Elara knew she couldn't meander. The threat had to be met head-on. She pulled out her manual for arcane physics, rapidly trying to overclock her mental capabilities. Her education had prepared her for this, a janissary of forgotten arts. She’d seen the risque diagrams of tentacle beasts and the velvety yet deadly spores of champignon in ancient texts. Now, a grotesque creature, like a muppet of nightmare made real, was at her deck. Its kiloton impact sent a shockwave through the ground. This was no filler monster; this was the genuine article, a gladiator of the wilds. The beast was not only a source of gloom but also a formidable foe, making her feel incredibly vulnerable. She had to ignite the seed of her power, to flip the script. This creature, a truly fantastic being of darkness, was jinxing the very fabric of reality around her apples orchard. With a final, desperate burst of stellar energy, Elara fired off a concentrated blast, forcing the creature to getaway. Its retreat was not without consequence; a noxious wasp-like swarm of microscopic germs had spread, necessitating a deep cleansing. She found herself exhausted, a bit drunk on adrenaline, contemplating the bizarre gender of her foe. The surrounding oxbows of the river unfolded peacefully, but the battle had left a lasting impression. She gathered her vocabulary to describe the event, the alliteration of her thoughts a strange comfort. The nearby warehouses, long abandoned, seemed to breathe a sigh of relief. This was her victory, hard-won, a testament to her years of weightlifting both physical and magical. She even felt a strange urge to rejoice, despite the ongoing homework of cleaning up the arcane residue. The latest telemetry readings confirmed the creature was gone, its presence now just a fading halberd in the memory, a reminder that the world held more than just packers and dialers.

She spent her days in the foundry, meticulously crafting bronze and golden artifacts. Her only real obligation now was to herself, an inner peace she worked hard to uplift. Sometimes, a visceral memory would tickle at the edges of her mind, a reminder of past journeys and the diverse faces she'd met. She was always thankful for the present, despite the gloom that occasionally threatened to unfold. Her home, a rustic cabin, was on the frontier, far from the mass of humanity. A familiar scent of hickory smoke clung to her vest, a comfort. She’d made many wishes in her youth, but now, simple contentment was her greatest nuggets. Today, she planned to decorate her small study, a quiet right of passage into a new, calmer phase of life.

Suddenly, a sudden tremor shook her cabin, more than just the usual thunders. Elara, always preoccupied with strange occurrences, quickly entered a state of heightened alert. She knew these lands harbored killers – not just the human lowlife variety, but something more enigmatic. Old stories of aliens and an antichrist figure, dismissed as indie mythology, now seemed chillingly plausible. A broken engine part, a strange item resembling a launcher, had been found near the perimeter of her isolated property just yesterday. Her aging bicycle lay nearby, its gaskets scratched and a tire broken, as if a hellhound had taken a bite. It was an underwhelming clue, though, for the scale of the impending doom. This wasn't some common tickler; this was something designed for physical action.

Elara knew she couldn't meander. The threat had to be met head-on. She pulled out her manual for arcane physics, rapidly trying to overclock her mental capabilities. Her education had prepared her for this, a janissary of forgotten arts. She’d seen the risque diagrams of tentacle beasts and the velvety yet deadly spores of champignon in ancient texts. Now, a grotesque creature, like a muppet of nightmare made real, was at her deck. Its kiloton impact sent a shockwave through the ground. This was no filler monster; this was the genuine article, a gladiator of the wilds. The beast was not only a source of gloom but also a formidable foe, making her feel incredibly vulnerable. She had to ignite the seed of her power, to flip the script. This creature, a truly fantastic being of darkness, was jinxing the very fabric of reality around her apples orchard.

With a final, desperate burst of stellar energy, Elara fired off a concentrated blast, forcing the creature to getaway. Its retreat was not without consequence; a noxious wasp-like swarm of microscopic germs had spread, necessitating a deep cleansing. This was her victory, hard-won, a testament to her years of weightlifting both physical and magical. rawPanelSentences: Elara, a veteran **alchemist** with graying temples, felt a strange **feeling** of unease, **though** her life had been largely **cool** since leaving the **warfare** behind. She spent her days in the **foundry**, meticulously crafting **bronze** and **golden** artifacts. Her only real **obligation** now was to herself, an inner peace she worked hard to **uplift**. Sometimes, a **visceral** memory would **tickle** at the edges of her mind, a reminder of past **journeys** and the **diverse** faces she'd met. She was always **thankful** for the present, despite the **gloom** that occasionally threatened to **unfold**. Her home, a rustic cabin, was on the **frontier**, far from the **mass** of humanity. A **familiar** **scent** of **hickory** smoke clung to her **vest**, a comfort. She’d made many **wishes** in her youth, but now, simple contentment was her greatest **nuggets**. Today, she planned to **decorate** her small study, a quiet **right** of passage into a new, calmer phase of life.

Suddenly, a **sudden** tremor shook her cabin, more than just the usual **thunders**. Elara, always **preoccupied** with strange occurrences, quickly **entered** a state of heightened alert. She knew these lands harbored **killers** – not just the human **lowlife** variety, but something more **enigmatic**. Old stories of **aliens** and an **antichrist** figure, dismissed as **indie** mythology, now seemed chillingly plausible. Rumors of an **undersized** yet deadly **porcupine**-like creature, twisted by **gangrene** and fueled by **impious** intent, had circulated among the **veterans**. A broken **engine** part, a strange **item** resembling a **launcher**, had been found near the **perimeter** of her isolated property just yesterday. Her aging **bicycle** lay nearby, its **gaskets** **scratched** and a tire **broken**, as if a **hellhound** had taken a bite. It was an **underwhelming** clue, **though**, for the scale of the impending **doom**. This wasn't some **common** **tickler**; this was something designed for **physical** **action**.

Elara knew she couldn't **meander**. The threat had to be met head-on. She pulled out her **manual** for arcane **physics**, rapidly trying to **overclock** her mental capabilities. Her **education** had prepared her for this, a **janissary** of forgotten arts. She’d seen the **risque** diagrams of **tentacle** beasts and the **velvety** yet deadly spores of **champignon** in ancient texts. Now, a grotesque creature, like a **muppet** of nightmare made real, was at her **deck**. Its **kiloton** impact sent a shockwave through the ground. This was no **filler** monster; this was the genuine article, a **gladiator** of the wilds. The beast was not only a source of **gloom** but also a formidable foe, making her feel incredibly **vulnerable**. She had to **ignite** the **seed** of her power, to **flip** the script. This creature, a truly **fantastic** being of darkness, was **jinxing** the very fabric of reality around her **apples** orchard.

With a final, desperate burst of **stellar** energy, Elara **fired** off a concentrated blast, forcing the creature to **getaway**. Its retreat was not without consequence; a noxious **wasp**-like swarm of microscopic **germs** had spread, necessitating a deep cleansing. She found herself exhausted, a bit **drunk** on adrenaline, contemplating the bizarre **gender** of her foe. The surrounding **oxbows** of the river **unfold**ed peacefully, but the battle had left a lasting impression. She gathered her **vocabulary** to describe the event, the **alliteration** of her thoughts a strange comfort. The nearby **warehouses**, long abandoned, seemed to breathe a sigh of relief. This was her victory, hard-won, a testament to her years of **weightlifting** both physical and magical. She even felt a strange urge to **rejoice**, despite the ongoing **homework** of cleaning up the arcane residue. The **latest** **telemetry** readings confirmed the creature was gone, its presence now just a fading **halberd** in the memory, a reminder that the world held more than just **packers** and **dialers**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic comic book style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. An adult woman with gray streaks in her long braided hair, a weathered face, and powerful arms, wearing a practical leather alchemist's vest over a work shirt. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara, an adult woman with gray streaks in her hair, stands contemplatively in her rustic foundry, surrounded by glowing bronze and golden artifacts, looking out a window at a distant frontier landscape, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, an adult woman with a furrowed brow, kneels beside her damaged bicycle, its gaskets scratched and tire broken, inspecting an unusual, metallic launcher-like item found nearby, close-up, dramatic low-angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara, an adult woman with powerful arms, stands defiantly, harnessing glowing magical energy in her hands, facing a grotesque, tentacled, muppet-like monster with gangrenous features at the edge of her wooden deck, wide shot, intense action pose. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, an adult woman with a weary but resolute expression, watches the retreating, indistinct silhouette of the monster vanish into a swirling cloud, surrounded by peaceful oxbows and distant warehouses, a sense of relief and exhaustion, full shot, golden hour lighting.

definitions.definition: The act of tickling.
An itchy feeling resembling the result of tickling.
definitions.example: I have a persistent tickle in my throat.

definitions.definition: A light tap of the ball.
A narrow strait.
To touch repeatedly or stroke delicately in a manner which causes laughter, pleasure and twitching.
definitions.example: He tickled Nancy's tummy, and she started to giggle.

definitions.definition: To unexpectedly touch or stroke delicately in a manner which causes displeasure or withdrawal.
definitions.example: A stranger tickled Nancy's tummy, causing her to scream in fear.

definitions.definition: (of a body part) To feel as if the body part in question is being tickled.
definitions.example: My nose tickles, and I'm going to sneeze!

definitions.definition: To appeal to someone's taste, curiosity etc.
To cause delight or amusement in.
definitions.example: He was tickled to receive such a wonderful gift.

definitions.definition: To feel titillation.
To catch fish in the hand (usually in rivers or smaller streams) by manually stimulating the fins.
To be excited or heartened.
Changeable, capricious; insecure.
A decorative border constructed from a continuous line, shaped into a repeated motif, that is commonly found in Greek art.
A structural motif in proteins consisting of four adjacent antiparallel strands and their linking loops.
One of the turns of a winding, crooked, or involved course.
definitions.example: the meanders of an old river, or of the veins and arteries in the body

definitions.definition: A tortuous or intricate movement.
One of a series of regular sinuous curves, bends, loops, turns, or windings in the channel of a river, stream, or other watercourse
Fretwork.
Perplexity.
A self-avoiding closed curve which intersects a line a number of times.
To wind or turn in a course or passage; to be intricate.
definitions.example: The stream meandered through the valley.

definitions.definition: To wind, turn, or twist; to make flexuous.
One who or that which kills.
definitions.example: Carbon monoxide is a silent killer.

definitions.definition: That which causes stress or is extremely difficult, especially that which may cause failure at a task.
definitions.example: That test was a killer.

definitions.definition: Something that is so far ahead of its competition that it effectively kills off that competition.
definitions.example: Digital streaming platforms are CD killers.

definitions.definition: A knockout form of darts or pool involving several players.
A club used for killing fish.
A particularly heavy type of handstamp, or portion of one, often obscuring a large part of the postage stamp.

A diacritical mark used in Indic scripts to suppress an inherent vowel (e.g., the Hindi viram, the Bengali or Oriya hasanta) or render the entire syllable silent (e.g., the Burmese virama, the Khmer toandakhiat).
A sea mammal related to dolphins and porpoises (Orcinus orca).
The act of binding oneself by a social, legal, or moral tie to someone.
A social, legal, or moral requirement, duty, contract, or promise that compels someone to follow or avoid a particular course of action.
A course of action imposed by society, law, or conscience by which someone is bound or restricted.
A legal agreement stipulating a specified action or forbearance by a party to the agreement; the document containing such agreement.
definitions.example: X shall be entitled to subcontract its obligation to provide the Support Services. <>

definitions.definition: An attendant spirit, often in animal or demon form.
definitions.example: The witch’s familiar was a black cat.

definitions.definition: A member of one's family or household.
A member of a pope's or bishop's household.
A close friend.
The officer of the Inquisition who arrested suspected people.
Known to one, or generally known; commonplace.
definitions.example: there’s a familiar face; that tune sounds familiar

definitions.definition: Acquainted.
definitions.example: I'm quite familiar with this system; she's not familiar with manual gears

definitions.definition: Intimate or friendly.
definitions.example: we are not on familiar terms; our neighbour is not familiar

definitions.definition: Inappropriately intimate or friendly.
definitions.example: Don’t be familiar with me, boy!

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to a family; familial.
The act or result of being uplifted.
A tectonic upheaval, especially one that takes place in the process of mountain building.
A brassiere that raises the breasts.
To raise something or someone to a higher physical, social, moral, intellectual, spiritual or emotional level.
(of a penalty) To aggravate; to increase.
(travel) To be accepted for carriage on a flight.
To remove (a child) from a damaging home environment by a social welfare organization.
To rub a surface with a sharp object, especially by a living creature to remove itching with nails, claws, etc.
definitions.example: Could you please scratch my back?

definitions.definition: To rub the skin with rough material causing a sensation of irritation; to cause itching.
definitions.example: I don't like that new scarf because it scratches my neck.

definitions.definition: To mark a surface with a sharp object, thereby leaving a scratch (noun).
definitions.example: A real diamond can easily scratch a pane of glass.

definitions.definition: To cross out, strike out, strike through some text on a page.
To produce a distinctive sound on a turntable by moving a vinyl record back and forth while manipulating the crossfader (see also scratching).
To commit a foul in pool, as where the cue ball is put into a pocket or jumps off the table.
definitions.example: Embarrassingly, he scratched on the break, popping the cue completely off the table.

definitions.definition: To score, not by skillful play but by some fortunate chance of the game.
To write or draw hastily or awkwardly; scrawl.
To dig or excavate with the claws.
definitions.example: Some animals scratch holes, in which they burrow.

definitions.definition: To dig or scrape (a person's skin) with claws or fingernails in self-defense or with the intention to injure.
definitions.example: The cat scratched the little girl.

definitions.definition: Produced by scratching.
A length of rope used for reefing a sail, or holding a stowed sail in place.
Any mechanical seal that serves to fill the space between two objects, generally to prevent leakage between the two objects while under compression.
A material which may be clamped between faces and acts as a static seal. Gaskets may be cut, formed, or molded to the desired configuration.
Any of a wide variety of seals or packings used between matched machine parts or around pipe joints to prevent the escape of a gas or fluid.
Despite that; however.
definitions.example: I will do it, though.

definitions.definition: (degree) Used to intensify statements or questions; indeed.
definitions.example: "Man, it's hot in here." — "Isn't it, though?"

definitions.definition: Despite the fact that; although.
definitions.example: Though it’s risky, it’s worth taking the chance.

definitions.definition: If, that, even if.
definitions.example: We shall be not sorry though the man die tonight.

definitions.definition: A set amount of travelling, seen as a single unit; a discrete trip, a voyage.
Any process or progression likened to a journey, especially one that involves difficulties or personal development.
definitions.example: my journey of dealing with grief

definitions.definition: A day.
A day's travelling; the distance travelled in a day.
A day's work.
The weight of finished coins delivered at one time to the Master of the Mint.
(collective) A group of giraffes.
To travel, to make a trip or voyage.
(Eastern US) An incompetent or foolish person.
definitions.example: Sally is such a muppet, the way she always misses the train.

definitions.definition: To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
definitions.example: You should knock before you enter, unless you want to see me naked.

definitions.definition: To cause to go (into), or to be received (into); to put in; to insert; to cause to be admitted.
definitions.example: to enter a knife into a piece of wood;  to enter a boy at college, a horse for a race, etc.

definitions.definition: To go or come into (a state or profession).
definitions.example: My twelve-year-old son will be entering his teens next year.  She had planned to enter the legal profession.

definitions.definition: To type (something) into a computer; to input.
definitions.example: Enter your user name and password.

definitions.definition: To record (something) in an account, ledger, etc.
To become a party to an agreement, treaty, etc.
To become effective; to come into effect.
To go into or upon, as lands, and take actual possession of them.
To place in regular form before the court, usually in writing; to put upon record in proper from and order.
definitions.example: to enter a writ, appearance, rule, or judgment

definitions.definition: To make report of (a vessel or its cargo) at the custom house; to submit a statement of (imported goods), with the original invoices, to the proper customs officer for estimating the duties. See entry.
To file, or register with the land office, the required particulars concerning (a quantity of public land) in order to entitle a person to a right of preemption.
To deposit for copyright the title or description of (a book, picture, map, etc.).
definitions.example: entered according to act of Congress

definitions.definition: To initiate; to introduce favourably.
Consisting of many different elements; various.
Different; dissimilar; distinct; not the same
Capable of various forms; multiform.

Composed of people with a variety of different demographic characteristics in terms of, for example, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, socioeconomic status, etc., and having a sizeable representation of people that are minorities in a given area.
Belonging to a minority group.
definitions.example: Idris Elba was a diverse hire for the franchise

definitions.definition: In different directions; diversely.
Of or relating to the viscera—internal organs of the body.
Having to do with the response of the body as opposed to the intellect, as in the distinction between feeling and thinking.
Having deep sensibility.

Any of several rodents of either of the taxonomic families Hystricidae (Old World porcupines) or Erethizontidae (New World porcupines), both from the infraorder Hystricognathi, noted for their sharp spines or quills, which are raised when the animal is attacked or surprised.
Not pious.
Lacking reverence or respect, especially towards God or a god.
Below the usual or expected size
Mutual good, shared by more than one.
A tract of land in common ownership; common land.
The people; the community.

The right of taking a profit in the land of another, in common either with the owner or with other persons; so called from the community of interest which arises between the claimant of the right and the owner of the soil, or between the claimants and other commoners entitled to the same right.
To communicate (something).
To converse, talk.
To have sex.
To participate.
To have a joint right with others in common ground.
To board together; to eat at a table in common.
Mutual; shared by more than one.
definitions.example: The two competitors have the common aim of winning the championship.

definitions.definition: Occurring or happening regularly or frequently; usual.
definitions.example: It is common to find sharks off this coast.

definitions.definition: Found in large numbers or in a large quantity; usual.
definitions.example: It differs from the common blackbird in the size of its beak.

definitions.definition: Simple, ordinary or vulgar.
(grammar) In some languages, particularly Germanic languages, of the gender originating from the coalescence of the masculine and feminine categories of nouns.
(grammar) Of or pertaining to common nouns as opposed to proper nouns.
Vernacular, referring to the name of a kind of plant or animal, i.e., common name vs. scientific name.
Profane; polluted.
Given to lewd habits; prostitute.
The waging of war or armed conflict against an enemy.
Military operations of some particular kind e.g. guerrilla warfare.
To lead a military life; to carry on continual wars.
A form of exercise in which weights are lifted.
A sport in which competitors lift heavy weights in two events: the snatch and the clean and jerk.
A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.
An expression of such a desire, often connected with ideas of magic and supernatural power.
The thing desired or longed for.
definitions.example: My dearest wish is to see them happily married.

definitions.definition: A water meadow.
To desire; to want.
definitions.example: I'll come tomorrow, if you wish it.

definitions.definition: To hope (+ object clause with may or in present subjunctive).
(followed by for) To hope (for a particular outcome).
(ditransitive) To bestow (a thought or gesture) towards (someone or something).
definitions.example: We wish you a Merry Christmas.

definitions.definition: (followed by to and an infinitive) To request or desire to do an activity.
To recommend; to seek confidence or favour on behalf of.
A small, compact chunk or clump.
definitions.example: a gold nugget

definitions.definition: A chicken nugget.
A tidbit of something valuable.
definitions.example: a nugget of wisdom

definitions.definition: A small piece of tasty food, a tidbit.
A type of boot polish.
A bud from the Cannabis sativa plant, especially one that is potent.
An inexperienced, newly trained fighter pilot.
A partial description gleaned from data mining.
One who or that which launches. A device that throws something or the person who initiates a launch.
definitions.example: The catapult made a good launcher for the small projectile.

definitions.definition: An application that launches another or others, often holding icons or menus for frequently used programs.
(physical) Matter, material.
A large quantity; a sum.
To form or collect into a mass; to form into a collective body; to bring together into masses; to assemble.
To have a certain mass.
definitions.example: I mass 70 kilograms.

definitions.definition: Involving a mass of things; concerning a large quantity or number.
definitions.example: There is evidence of mass extinctions in the distant past.

definitions.definition: Involving a mass of people; of, for, or by the masses.
definitions.example: Mass unemployment resulted from the financial collapse.

definitions.definition: A person or device that dials, as using a telephone.
definitions.example: He treated his assistant as a voice-operated dialer.

definitions.definition: Any raised flat surface that can be walked on: a balcony; a porch; a raised patio; a flat rooftop.
The floorlike covering of the horizontal sections, or compartments, of a ship. Small vessels have only one deck; larger ships have two or three decks.
definitions.example: to swab the deck

definitions.definition: A main aeroplane surface, especially of a biplane or multiplane.
A pack or set of playing cards.
(by extension) A set of cards owned by each individual player and from which they draw when playing.
A set of slides for a presentation.
A heap or store.
A folded paper used for distributing illicit drugs.
The floor.
definitions.example: We hit the deck as bullets began to fly.

definitions.definition: To furnish with a deck, as a vessel.
To knock someone to the floor, especially with a single punch.
definitions.example: Wow, did you see her deck that guy who pinched her?

definitions.definition: To cause a player to run out of cards to draw and usually lose the game as a result.
A U-shaped piece of wood used as a collar for an ox, the upper parts fastened to its yoke
A meander in a river; the land enclosed by such a loop
To run a processor (CPU), or any electronic logic device, at a speed higher than is recommended by the manufacturer.

A facility that melts metals in special furnaces and pours the molten metal into molds to make products. Foundries are usually specified according to the type of metal dealt with: iron foundry, brass foundry, etc.
The act, process, or art of casting metals; founding.
A semiconductor fabrication plant in the microelectronics industry.
An independent publisher.
A work released by an independent publisher.
A type of rock music, generally soft-style without screaming or aggression, mixed with synthesized music and electronic.
A person who listens to indie music and follows indie fashion.
Independent; from outside the mainstream.
definitions.example: an indie pop group

definitions.definition: An unexpected occurrence; a surprise.
Happening quickly and with little or no warning.
definitions.example: The sudden drop in temperature left everyone cold and confused.

definitions.definition: Hastily prepared or employed; quick; rapid.
Hasty; violent; rash; precipitate.
Suddenly.
A fertilized and ripened ovule, containing an embryonic plant.
Any small seed-like fruit.
definitions.example: If you plant a seed in the spring, you may have a pleasant surprise in the autumn.

definitions.definition: Any propagative portion of a plant which may be sown, such as true seeds, seed-like fruits, tubers, or bulbs.
(collective) An amount of seeds that cannot be readily counted.
definitions.example: The entire field was covered with geese eating the freshly sown seed.

definitions.definition: A fragment of coral.
Semen.
definitions.example: A man must use his seed to start and raise a family.

definitions.definition: A precursor.
definitions.example: the seed of an idea; which idea was the seed (idea)?

definitions.definition: The initial state, condition or position of a changing, growing or developing process; the ultimate precursor in a defined chain of precursors.
Offspring, descendants, progeny.
definitions.example: the seed of Abraham

definitions.definition: Race; generation; birth.
A small bubble formed in imperfectly fused glass.
To plant or sow an area with seeds.
definitions.example: I seeded my lawn with bluegrass.

definitions.definition: To cover thinly with something scattered; to ornament with seedlike decorations.
To start; to provide, assign or determine the initial resources for, position of, state of.
definitions.example: A venture capitalist seeds young companies.

definitions.definition: To allocate a seeding to a competitor.
To leave (files) available for others to download through peer-to-peer file sharing protocols (e.g. BitTorrent).
To be qualified to compete, especially in a quarter-final, semi-final or final.
definitions.example: The tennis player seeded into the quarters.

definitions.definition: To produce seed.
To grow to maturity.
To ejaculate inside the penetratee during intercourse, especially in the rectum.
A moderate or refreshing state of cold; moderate temperature of the air between hot and cold; coolness.
definitions.example: in the cool of the morning

definitions.definition: A calm temperament.
The property of being cool, popular or in fashion.
Having a slightly low temperature; mildly or pleasantly cold.
Allowing or suggesting heat relief.
definitions.example: Linen has made cool and breathable clothing for millennia.

definitions.definition: Of a color, in the range of violet to green.
definitions.example: If you have a reddish complexion, you should mainly wear cool colors.

definitions.definition: Of a person, not showing emotion; calm and in control of oneself.
Unenthusiastic, lukewarm, skeptical.
definitions.example: His proposals had a cool reception.

definitions.definition: Calmly audacious.
definitions.example: In control as always, he came up with a cool plan.

definitions.definition: Applied facetiously to a sum of money, commonly as if to give emphasis to the largeness of the amount.
Of a person, knowing what to do and how to behave; considered popular by others.
In fashion, part of or fitting the in crowd; originally hipster slang.
Of an action, all right; acceptable; that does not present a problem.
definitions.example: Is it cool if I sleep here tonight?

definitions.definition: Of a person, not upset by circumstances that might ordinarily be upsetting.
definitions.example: I'm completely cool with my girlfriend leaving me.

definitions.definition: Quietly impudent, defiant, or selfish; deliberately presuming: said of persons and acts.
Kyphosus vaigiensis, a fish found in southeast Asia.
Made of, or relating to, gold.
definitions.example: She wore a golden crown.

definitions.definition: Having a colour or other richness suggestive of gold.
definitions.example: Under a golden sun.

definitions.definition: Of a beverage, flavoured or colored with turmeric.
Marked by prosperity, creativity etc.
definitions.example: The Renaissance was a golden era.

definitions.definition: Advantageous or very favourable.
definitions.example: This is a golden opportunity

definitions.definition: Relating to a fiftieth anniversary.
definitions.example: It's not long until our golden wedding.

definitions.definition: Relating to the elderly or retired.
definitions.example: After retiring, Bob and Judy moved to Arizona to live out their golden years.

definitions.definition: Fine, without problems.
A naturally occurring or man-made alloy of copper, usually in combination with tin, but also with one or more other metals.
A reddish-brown colour, the colour of bronze.
A work of art made of bronze, especially a sculpture.
A bronze medal.
definitions.example: She wanted to win the tournament, but had to settle for the bronze after being beaten in the semi-finals.

definitions.definition: Boldness; impudence; brass.
To plate with bronze.
definitions.example: My mother bronzed my first pair of baby shoes.

definitions.definition: To color bronze; (of the sun) to tan.
(of the skin) To change to a bronze or tan colour due to exposure to the sun.
To make hard or unfeeling; to brazen.
Made of bronze metal.
Having a reddish-brown colour.
(of the skin) Tanned; darkened as a result of exposure to the sun.
One who tickles.
A person who or thing which amuses or excites.
A reminder.
definitions.example: Put it in the tickler file for next week.

definitions.definition: A latex condom that has additional protrusions, for enhancing the sexual pleasure of the user.
Something puzzling or difficult; a conundrum.
A tickler file, file cabinet, or similar containing memoranda of deadlines arranged in date order.
A prong used by coopers to extract bungs from casks.
A dram of spirits.
Showing appreciation or gratitude.
definitions.example: I'm thankful that you helped me out today. How can I ever repay you?

definitions.definition: Obtaining or deserving thanks; thankworthy.
One who works against the teachings of Christ.
Any life form of extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin.
A person, animal, plant, or other thing which is from outside the family, group, organization, or territory under consideration.
A foreigner residing in a country.
One excluded from certain privileges; one alienated or estranged.
To estrange; to alienate.
To transfer the ownership of something.
A handbook.
A booklet that instructs on the usage of a particular machine or product.
definitions.example: The dishwasher isn't working; can you remember where we put the manual?

definitions.definition: A drill in the use of weapons, etc.
An old office-book like the modern Roman Catholic ritual.
A keyboard for the hands on a harpsichord, organ, or other musical instrument.
A manual transmission; a gearbox, especially of a motorized vehicle, shifted by the operator.
(by synecdoche) A vehicle with a manual transmission.
A bicycle technique whereby the front wheel is held aloft by the rider, without the use of pedal force.
Manual measurement of the blood pressure, done with a manual sphygmomanometer.
definitions.example: do a manual

definitions.definition: Pertaining to an enigma.
Mysterious.
Defying description.
(variant) Enigmatical.
Destiny, especially terrible.
An undesirable fate; an impending severe occurrence or danger that seems inevitable.
A feeling of danger, impending danger, darkness or despair.
A law.
A judgment or decision.
A sentence or penalty for illegal behaviour.
Death.
definitions.example: They met an untimely doom when the mineshaft caved in.

definitions.definition: (sometimes capitalized) The Last Judgment; or, an artistic representation thereof.
To pronounce sentence or judgment on; to condemn.
definitions.example: a criminal doomed to death

definitions.definition: To destine; to fix irrevocably the ill fate of.
To judge; to estimate or determine as a judge.
To ordain as a penalty; hence, to mulct or fine.
To assess a tax upon, by estimate or at discretion.
A person with long experience of a particular activity.
A group, animal, etc. with long experience of a particular activity.
A person who has served in the armed forces, especially an old soldier who has seen long service; also called a war veteran to distinguish from veterans that weren't in armed conflict.
A medicine or drug, especially a cathartic or purgative.
The art or profession of healing disease; medicine.
Natural philosophy; physics.
A physician.
To cure or heal.
To administer medicine to, especially a purgative.
The branch of science concerned with the study of the properties and interactions of space, time, matter and energy.
definitions.example: Newtonian physics was extended by Einstein to explain the effects of travelling near the speed of light; quantum physics extends it to account for the behaviour of atoms.

definitions.definition: The physical aspects of a phenomenon or a system, especially those studied scientifically.
definitions.example: The physics of car crashes would not let Tom Cruise walk away like that.

definitions.definition: Physical examination.
definitions.example: How long has it been since your last physical?

definitions.definition: Of medicine.
Of matter or nature.
Of the human body.
(heading) To use or experience the sense of touch.
(heading) To sense or think emotionally or judgmentally.
To be or become aware of.
To experience the consequences of.
definitions.example: Feel my wrath!

definitions.definition: To seem (through touch or otherwise).
definitions.example: It looks like wood, but it feels more like plastic.

definitions.definition: To understand.
definitions.example: I don't want you back here, ya feel me?

definitions.definition: Sensation, particularly through the skin.
definitions.example: The wool on my arm produced a strange feeling.

definitions.definition: Emotion; impression.
definitions.example: The house gave me a feeling of dread.

definitions.definition: (always in the plural) Emotional state or well-being.
definitions.example: You really hurt my feelings when you said that.

definitions.definition: (always in the plural) Emotional attraction or desire.
definitions.example: Many people still have feelings for their first love.

definitions.definition: Intuition.
definitions.example: He has no feeling for what he can say to somebody in such a fragile emotional condition.

definitions.definition: An opinion, an attitude.
Emotionally sensitive.
definitions.example: Despite the rough voice, the coach is surprisingly feeling.

definitions.definition: Expressive of great sensibility; attended by, or evincing, sensibility.
definitions.example: He made a feeling representation of his wrongs.

definitions.definition: To set fire to (something), to light (something)
To spark off (something), to trigger
To commence burning.
To subject to the action of intense heat; to heat strongly; often said of incombustible or infusible substances.
definitions.example: to ignite iron or platinum

definitions.definition: (notcomp) Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of stars.
Heavenly.
(by extension) Exceptional.
definitions.example: The actress gave a stellar performance.

definitions.definition: The small mass of cells from which a new organism develops; a seed, bud or spore.
A pathogenic microorganism.
The embryo of a seed, especially of a seed used as a cereal or grain. See Wikipedia article on cereal germ.
The origin of an idea or project.
definitions.example: the germ of civil liberty

definitions.definition: An equivalence class that includes a specified function defined in an open neighborhood.
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.
definitions.example: to break flax

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.
definitions.example: to break into a run or gallop

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
Fragmented, in separate pieces.
(of a promise, etc) Breached; violated; not kept.
definitions.example: broken promises of neutrality

definitions.definition: Non-functional; not functioning properly.
definitions.example: I think my doorbell is broken.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Completely defeated and dispirited; shattered; destroyed.
definitions.example: The bankruptcy and divorce, together with the death of his son, left him completely broken.

definitions.definition: Having no money; bankrupt, broke.
(of land) Uneven.
(of a tactic or option) Overpowered; overly powerful; too powerful.
The loud rumbling, cracking, or crashing sound caused by expansion of rapidly heated air around a lightning bolt.
definitions.example: Thunder is preceded by lightning.

definitions.definition: A deep, rumbling noise resembling thunder.
definitions.example: Off in the distance, he heard the thunder of hoofbeats, signalling a stampede.

definitions.definition: An alarming or startling threat or denunciation.
The discharge of electricity; a thunderbolt.
The spotlight.
definitions.example: Shortly after I announced my pregnancy, he stole my thunder with his news of landing his dream job.

definitions.definition: To produce thunder; to sound, rattle, or roar, as a discharge of atmospheric electricity; often used impersonally.
definitions.example: It thundered continuously.

definitions.definition: To make a noise like thunder.
definitions.example: The train thundered along the tracks.

definitions.definition: To talk with a loud, threatening voice.
To say (something) with a loud, threatening voice.
definitions.example: "Get back to work at once!", he thundered.

definitions.definition: To produce something with incredible power
A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.

Any of various tree-borne fruits or vegetables especially considered as resembling an apple; also (with qualifying words) used to form the names of other specific fruits such as custard apple, rose apple, thorn apple etc.
The fruit of the Tree of Knowledge, eaten by Adam and Eve according to post-Biblical Christian tradition; the forbidden fruit.
A tree of the genus Malus, especially one cultivated for its edible fruit; the apple tree.
The wood of the apple tree.
(in the plural) Short for apples and pears, slang for stairs.
The ball in baseball.
When smiling, the round, fleshy part of the cheeks between the eyes and the corners of the mouth.
A Native American or red-skinned person who acts and/or thinks like a white (Caucasian) person.
(ice hockey slang) An assist.
To become apple-like.
To form buds.
One who practices alchemy.
One who blends material or substances in the nature or supposed nature of alchemy.
Work that is done at home, especially school exercises assigned by a teacher.
definitions.example: You must do your homework before you can watch television.

definitions.definition: Preliminary or preparatory work, such as research.
definitions.example: The speaker had certainly done his homework before delivering the lecture.

definitions.definition: Housework.
A restraint or support, especially one consisting of a loop or network of rope or straps.
A collection of wires or cables bundled and routed according to their function.
The complete dress, especially in a military sense, of a man or a horse; armour in general.
The part of a loom comprising the heddles, with their means of support and motion, by which the threads of the warp are alternately raised and depressed for the passage of the shuttle.
Equipment for any kind of labour.
To place a harness on something; to tie up or restrain.
definitions.example: They harnessed the horse to the post.

definitions.definition: To capture, control or put to use.
definitions.example: Imagine what might happen if it were possible to harness solar energy fully.

definitions.definition: To equip with armour.

An infantry soldier, often of Christian descent and forcibly converted to Islam, in a former elite Turkish (Ottoman) guard (disbanded in 1826); by extension, any Turkish soldier, particularly one escorting a traveller.
An elite, highly loyal supporter.
To cast a spell on.
To bring bad luck to.
To cause something to happen by mentioning it, usually sarcastically.
To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
One who is intoxicated with alcohol.
A habitual drinker, especially one who is frequently intoxicated.
A drinking-bout; a period of drunkenness.
A drunken state.
Intoxicated as a result of excessive alcohol consumption, usually by drinking alcoholic beverages.
Habitually or frequently in a state of intoxication.
(usually followed by with or on) Elated or emboldened.
definitions.example: Drunk with power he immediately ordered a management reshuffle.

definitions.definition: Drenched or saturated with moisture or liquid.
A person whose business is to pack things; especially, one who packs food for preservation
definitions.example: He works as a pork packer.

definitions.definition: A software program that compresses code or data.
A ring of packing or a special device to render gastight and watertight the space between the tubing and bore of an oil well.
An artificial penis or similar object worn by a drag king, trans man, etc., inside the trousers.
An object inserted to hold a space open for the purpose of alignment; a spacer or shim.
A kind of trunk for luggage.
A fanciful or whimsical person.
Existing in or constructed from fantasy; of or relating to fantasy; fanciful.
definitions.example: He told fantastic stories of dragons and goblins.

definitions.definition: Not believable; implausible; seemingly only possible in fantasy.
definitions.example: She entered the lab and stood gaping for a good ten minutes at the fantastic machinery at work all around her.

definitions.definition: Resembling fantasies in irregularity, caprice, or eccentricity; irregular; grotesque.
Wonderful; marvelous; excellent; extraordinarily good or great (used especially as an intensifier).
definitions.example: "I had a simply fantastic vacation, and I can't wait to tell you all about it!"

definitions.definition: One who fills.
Something added to fill a space or add weight or size.
Any semisolid substance used to fill gaps, cracks or pores.
A relatively inert ingredient added to modify physical characteristics.
A short article in a newspaper or magazine.
A short piece of music or an announcement between radio or TV programmes.
Any spoken sound or word used to fill gaps in speech; filled pause.
Cut tobacco used to make up the body of a cigar.
In COBOL, the description of an unnamed part of a record that contains no data relevant to a given context (normally capitalised when in a data division).
A plant that lacks a distinctive shape and can fill inconvenient spaces around other plants in pots or gardens.
(usually in the plural) Any standing tree or standard higher than the surrounding coppice in the form of forest known as "coppice under standards".
In functional programming, a kind of higher-order function that is the opposite of a fold.
To undo a folding.
definitions.example: to unfold a map; to unfold a tablecloth; she unpacks the new dress and unfolds it carefully

definitions.definition: To turn out; to happen; to develop.
To reveal.
To open (anything covered or closed); to lay open to view or contemplation; to bring out in all the details, or by successive development.
definitions.example: to unfold one's designs;  to unfold the principles of a science

definitions.definition: To release from a fold or pen.
definitions.example: to unfold sheep

definitions.definition: Suggestive of sexual impropriety; bordering on the indelicate.
The necrosis or rotting of flesh, usually caused by lack of blood supply.
definitions.example: If gangrene sets in, we may have to amputate the foot.

definitions.definition: A damaging or corrupting influence.
To produce gangrene in.
To be affected with gangrene.
To corrupt; To cause to become degenerate.
Any of many types of stinging flying insect resembling a hornet.
Any of the members of suborder Apocrita, excepting the ants (family Formicidae) and bees (clade Anthophila).
A person who behaves in an angry or insolent way, hence waspish.
(applied sciences) the science, and associated technology, of the automatic recording and transmission of data from a remote source to a receiving station for analysis
The repetition of consonant sounds at the beginning of two or more words immediately succeeding each other, or at short intervals.
The recurrence of the same letter in accented parts of words, as in Anglo-Saxon alliterative meter.
A hand weapon consisting of a long pole fitted with a metal head; the head consists of a blade similar to an axe and usually a spike or hook.
Any of various deciduous hardwood trees of the genus Carya or Annamocarya.
The wood of these trees.
Of or pertaining to the hickory tree or its wood.
Near the end of a period of time.
definitions.example: It was late in the evening when we finally arrived.

definitions.definition: Specifically, near the end of the day.
definitions.example: It was getting late and I was tired.

definitions.definition: (usually not comparable) Associated with the end of a period.
definitions.example: Late Latin is less fully inflected than classical Latin.

definitions.definition: Not arriving until after an expected time.
definitions.example: Even though we drove as fast as we could, we were still late.

definitions.definition: Not having had an expected menstrual period.
definitions.example: I'm late, honey. Could you buy a test?

definitions.definition: Deceased, dead: used particularly when speaking of the dead person's actions while alive. (Often used with "the"; see usage notes.)
definitions.example: Her late husband had left her well provided for.

definitions.definition: Existing or holding some position not long ago, but not now; departed, or gone out of office.
definitions.example: the late administration

definitions.definition: Recent — relative to the noun it modifies.
Of a star or class of stars, cooler than the sun.
After a deadline has passed, past a designated time.
definitions.example: We drove as fast as we could, but we still arrived late.

definitions.definition: Formerly, especially in the context of service in a military unit.
definitions.example: Colonel Easterwood, late of the 34th Carbines, was a guest at the dinner party.

definitions.definition: Not long ago; just now.
The most recent thing, particularly information or news.
definitions.example: Have you heard the latest?

definitions.definition: A large construction used in warfare, such as a battering ram, catapult etc.
A tool; a utensil or implement.
A complex mechanical device which converts energy into useful motion or physical effects.
A person or group of people which influence a larger group; a driving force.
The part of a car or other vehicle which provides the force for motion, now especially one powered by internal combustion.
A self-powered vehicle, especially a locomotive, used for pulling cars along a track.
A software or hardware system responsible for a specific technical task (usually with qualifying word).
definitions.example: a graphics engine

definitions.definition: Ingenuity; cunning, trickery, guile.
The result of cunning; something ingenious, a contrivance; (in negative senses) a plot, a scheme.
Natural talent; genius.
Anything used to effect a purpose; any device or contrivance; an agent.
To equip with an engine; said especially of steam vessels.
definitions.example: Vessels are often built by one firm and engined by another.

definitions.definition: To assault with an engine.
To contrive; to put into action.
To rack; to torture.
A measure of the strength of an explosion or a bomb based on how many thousand tons of TNT would be needed to produce the same energy.
definitions.example: That's a 300-kiloton nuclear warhead.

definitions.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
definitions.example: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.

definitions.definition: More likely to be exposed to malicious programs or viruses.
definitions.example: a vulnerable PC with no antivirus software

definitions.definition: The part of a country which borders or faces another country or unsettled region
The most advanced or recent version of something; leading edge.
definitions.example: the frontier of civilization

definitions.definition: An outwork of a fortification.
To live as pioneers on frontier territory.
To place on the frontier.
To set (something, often a building) on fire.
To heat as with fire, but without setting on fire, as ceramic, metal objects, etc.
definitions.example: If you fire the pottery at too high a temperature, it may crack.

definitions.definition: To drive away by setting a fire.
To terminate the employment contract of (an employee), especially for cause (such as misconduct or poor performance).
To shoot (a gun or analogous device).
definitions.example: He fired his radar gun at passing cars.

definitions.definition: To shoot a gun, cannon, or similar weapon.
definitions.example: Don't fire until you see the whites of their eyes.

definitions.definition: To shoot; to attempt to score a goal.
To cause an action potential in a cell.
definitions.example: When a neuron fires, it transmits information.

definitions.definition: To forcibly direct (something).
definitions.example: He answered the questions the reporters fired at him.

definitions.definition: (computer sciences, software engineering) To initiate an event (by means of an event handler).
definitions.example: The event handler should only fire after all web page content has finished loading.

definitions.definition: To inflame; to irritate, as the passions.
definitions.example: to fire the soul with anger, pride, or revenge

definitions.definition: To animate; to give life or spirit to.
definitions.example: to fire the genius of a young man

definitions.definition: To feed or serve the fire of.
definitions.example: to fire a boiler

definitions.definition: To light up as if by fire; to illuminate.
To cauterize.
To catch fire; to be kindled.
To be irritated or inflamed with passion.
Dismissed, let go from a job.
Heated in a furnace, kiln, etc., to become permanently hardened.
Agaricus bisporus, a species of mushroom commonly used in cooking
Class; kind.
(grammar) A division of nouns and pronouns (and sometimes of other parts of speech) into masculine or feminine, and sometimes other categories like neuter or common, and animate or inanimate.
(now sometimes proscribed) Sex (a category such as "male" or "female" into which sexually-reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species).
definitions.example: The effect of the medication is dependent upon age, gender, and other factors.

definitions.definition: Identification as a man, a woman or something else, and association with a (social) role or set of behavioral and cultural traits, clothing, etc; a category to which a person belongs on this basis. (Compare gender role, gender identity.)

(hardware) The quality which distinguishes connectors, which may be male (fitting into another connector) and female (having another connector fit into it), or genderless/androgynous (capable of fitting together with another connector of the same type).
To assign a gender to (a person); to perceive as having a gender; to address using terms (pronouns, nouns, adjectives...) that express a certain gender.
To perceive (a thing) as having characteristics associated with a certain gender, or as having been authored by someone of a certain gender.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
definitions.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.

definitions.definition: Sound made through vibration of the vocal cords; sonant, or intonated, utterance; tone; — distinguished from mere breath sound as heard in whispering and voiceless consonants.
The tone or sound emitted by an object
The faculty or power of utterance
definitions.example: to cultivate the voice

definitions.definition: That which is communicated; message; meaning.
An expressed opinion, choice, will, desire, or wish; the right or ability to make such expression or to have it considered
Command; precept.
One who speaks; a speaker.
A particular style or way of writing that expresses a certain tone or feeling.

(grammar) A particular way of inflecting or conjugating verbs, or a particular form of a verb, by means of which is indicated the relation of the subject of the verb to the action which the verb expresses.
definitions.example: The verbal system of Latin has two voices, active and passive.

definitions.definition: In harmony, an independent vocal or instrumental part in a piece of composition.
definitions.example: The theme of this piece constantly migrates between the three voice parts.

definitions.definition: (IRC) A flag associated with a user on a channel, determining whether or not they can send messages to the channel.
An elongated, boneless, flexible organ or limb of some animals, such as the octopus and squid.
One of the glandular hairs on the leaves of certain insectivorous plants.
An insidious reach or influence.
definitions.example: the tentacles of the criminal underworld

definitions.definition: A demonic dog of hell, typically of unnatural size, strength or speed, with black fur, glowing eyes, and ghostly or phantom characteristics.
The process of imparting knowledge, skill and judgment.
definitions.example: 2016-06-17 AROP JOSEPH "Education is the slight hammer that breaks the yoke of ignorance, and moulds knowledge, skills, ideas, good moral values in a person be it a child, a youth or full grown adult. no matter a persons age learning never stops".

definitions.definition: Facts, skills and ideas that have been learned, either formally or informally.
definitions.example: He has had a classical education.

definitions.definition: A loose robe or outer garment worn historically by men in Arab or Middle Eastern countries.
A sleeveless garment that buttons down the front, worn over a shirt, and often as part of a suit; a waistcoat.
A sleeveless garment, often with a low-cut neck, usually worn under a shirt or blouse.
A sleeveless top, typically with identifying colours or logos, worn by an athlete or member of a sports team.
Any sleeveless outer garment, often for a purpose such as identification, safety, or storage.
A vestment.
Clothing generally; array; garb.
To clothe with, or as with, a vestment, or garment; to dress; to robe; to cover, surround, or encompass closely.
To clothe with authority, power, etc.; to put in possession; to invest; to furnish; to endow; followed by with and the thing conferred.
definitions.example: to vest a court with power to try cases of life and death

definitions.definition: To place or give into the possession or discretion of some person or authority; to commit to another; with in before the possessor.
definitions.example: The power of life and death is vested in the king, or in the courts.

definitions.definition: To invest; to put.
definitions.example: to vest money in goods, land, or houses

definitions.definition: To clothe with possession; also, to give a person an immediate fixed right of present or future enjoyment of.
definitions.example: an estate is vested in possession

definitions.definition: (of an inheritance or a trust fund) To devolve upon the person currently entitled when a prior interest has ended.
definitions.example: Upon the death of the Sovereign the Crown automatically vests in the next heir without the need of coronation or other formality.

definitions.definition: (financial) To become vested, to become permanent.
definitions.example: My pension vests at the end of the month and then I can take it with me when I quit.

definitions.definition: A maneuver which rotates an object end over end.
definitions.example: The diver did a couple of flips before landing in the pool.

definitions.definition: A complete change of direction, decision, movement etc.
A slingshot.
A hairstyle popular among boys in the 1960s–70s and 2000s–10s, in which the hair goes halfway down the ears, at which point it sticks out
definitions.example: Justin Bieber and Zac Efron are among the celebrities who wore a flip.

definitions.definition: The purchase of an asset (usually a house) which is then improved and sold quickly for profit.
To throw so as to turn over.
definitions.example: You need to flip the pancake onto the other side.

definitions.definition: To put into a quick revolving motion through a snap of the thumb and index finger.
definitions.example: If you can't decide which option to go for, flip a coin.

definitions.definition: To win a state (or county) won by another party in the preceding elections
definitions.example: Wisconsin had been Democratic for decades, but the Republicans flipped it in 2016.

definitions.definition: To turn state's evidence; to agree to testify against one's co-conspirators in exchange for concessions from prosecutors.
definitions.example: The mafioso flipped on his superiors to get a lighter sentence.

definitions.definition: To induce someone to turn state's evidence; to get someone to agree to testify against their co-conspirators in exchange for concessions.
definitions.example: The district attorney was able to strengthen his case against the bank robber by flipping the getaway driver.

definitions.definition: To go berserk or crazy.
definitions.example: I'd flip if anyone broke my phone.

definitions.definition: To buy an asset (usually a house), improve it and sell it quickly for profit.
To invert a bit (binary digit), changing it from 0 to 1 or from 1 to 0.
A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
Likewise
Failing to interest; not as exciting as promised or expected.
A distinctive odour or smell.
definitions.example: the scent of a skunk

definitions.definition: An odour left by an animal that may be used for tracing.
definitions.example: The dogs lost the scent.

definitions.definition: The sense of smell.
definitions.example: I believe the bloodhound has the best scent of all dogs.

definitions.definition: A perfume.
Any trail or trace that can be followed to find something or someone, such as the paper left behind in a paperchase.
Sense, perception.
To detect the scent of; to discern by the sense of smell.
definitions.example: The hounds scented the fox in the woods.

definitions.definition: To have a suspicion of.
definitions.example: I scented trouble when I saw them running down the hill towards me.

definitions.definition: To impart an odour to.
definitions.example: Scent the air with burning sage before you begin your meditation.

definitions.definition: To have a smell.
To hunt animals by means of the sense of smell.
Straight, not bent.
definitions.example: a right line

definitions.definition: Of an angle, having a size of 90 degrees, or one quarter of a complete rotation; the angle between two perpendicular lines.
definitions.example: The kitchen counter formed a right angle with the back wall.

definitions.definition: Of a geometric figure, incorporating a right angle between edges, faces, axes, etc.
definitions.example: a right triangle, a right prism, a right cone

definitions.definition: Complying with justice, correctness or reason; correct, just, true.
definitions.example: I thought you'd made a mistake, but it seems you were right all along.

definitions.definition: Appropriate, perfectly suitable; fit for purpose.
definitions.example: Is this the right software for my computer?

definitions.definition: Healthy, sane, competent.
definitions.example: I'm afraid my father is no longer in his right mind.

definitions.definition: Real; veritable (used emphatically).
definitions.example: You've made a right mess of the kitchen!

definitions.definition: All right; not requiring assistance.
Most favourable or convenient; fortunate.
Designating the side of the body which is positioned to the east if one is facing north. This arrow points to the reader's right: →
definitions.example: After the accident, her right leg was slightly shorter than her left.

definitions.definition: Designed to be placed or worn outward.
definitions.example: the right side of a piece of cloth

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the political right; conservative.
(in ancient Rome) A person (professional or slave) who entertained the public by engaging in mortal combat with another, or with a wild animal.
(by extension) A disputant in a public controversy or debate.
A professional boxer.
To fight as entertainment for others.
To compete in a public contest.
To debate or argue.
To act aggressively toward others.
A means of escape.
The effecting of an escape.
A vacation or holiday, or the destination for one.
Pertaining to an escape, as in a vehicle or plans.
definitions.example: They'd been discussing their getaway plans for weeks.

definitions.definition: One who causes trouble, especially one who does so deliberately.
A complainer.
Something done so as to accomplish a purpose.
A way of motion or functioning.
definitions.example: Knead bread with a rocking action.

definitions.definition: Fast-paced activity.
definitions.example: a movie full of exciting action

definitions.definition: A mechanism; a moving part or assembly.
definitions.example: a rifle action

definitions.definition: The mechanism, that is the set of moving mechanical parts, of a keyboard instrument, like a piano, which transfers the motion of the key to the sound-making device.
The distance separating the strings and the fretboard on a guitar.
Sexual intercourse.
definitions.example: She gave him some action.

definitions.definition: Combat.
definitions.example: He saw some action in the Korean War.

definitions.definition: A charge or other process in a law court (also called lawsuit and actio).

A mapping from a pairing of mathematical objects to one of them, respecting their individual structures. The pairing is typically a Cartesian product or a tensor product. The object that is not part of the output is said to act on the other object. In any given context, action is used as an abbreviation for a more fully named notion, like group action or left group action.
The product of energy and time, especially the product of the Lagrangian and time.
The event or connected series of events, either real or imaginary, forming the subject of a play, poem, or other composition; the unfolding of the drama of events.
(painting and sculpture) The attitude or position of the several parts of the body as expressive of the sentiment or passion depicted.
Spin put on the bowling ball.
A share in the capital stock of a joint-stock company, or in the public funds.
To act on a request etc, in order to put it into effect.
To initiate a legal action against someone.
Demanding or signifying the start of something, usually an act or scene of a theatric performance.
definitions.example: The director yelled ‘Action!’ before the camera started rolling.

definitions.definition: Like velvet; soft, smooth, soothing.
definitions.example: The crooner had a velvety voice that made the ladies swoon.

definitions.definition: A usually alphabetized and explained collection of words e.g. of a particular field, or prepared for a specific purpose, often for learning.
The collection of words a person knows and uses.
definitions.example: My Russian vocabulary is very limited.

definitions.definition: The stock of words used in a particular field.
definitions.example: The vocabulary of social sciences is often incomprehensible to ordinary people.

definitions.definition: The words of a language collectively.
definitions.example: The vocabulary of any language is influenced by contacts with other cultures.

definitions.definition: A range of artistic or stylistic forms or techniques
The sum of the distance of all the lengths of the sides of an object.
The length of such a boundary.
The outer limits of an area.
A fortified strip or boundary usually protecting a military position.
An instrument for determining the extent and shape of the field of vision.
Concerned with something else; distracted; giving one's attention elsewhere.
definitions.example: I was preoccupied with a deadline at work, and I forgot his birthday.

definitions.definition: Describing a scientific name that was previously used, a junior homonym.
To distract; to occupy or draw attention elsewhere.
definitions.example: The father tried to preoccupy the child with his keys.

definitions.definition: To occupy or take possession of beforehand.
To be very happy, be delighted, exult; to feel joy.
To have (someone) as a lover or spouse; to enjoy sexually.
To make happy, exhilarate.
To enjoy.
Darkness, dimness or obscurity.
definitions.example: the gloom of a forest, or of midnight

definitions.definition: A melancholic, depressing or despondent atmosphere.
Cloudiness or heaviness of mind; melancholy; aspect of sorrow; low spirits; dullness.
A drying oven used in gunpowder manufacture.
To be dark or gloomy.
To look or feel sad, sullen or despondent.
To render gloomy or dark; to obscure; to darken.
To fill with gloom; to make sad, dismal, or sullen.
To shine or appear obscurely or imperfectly; to glimmer.
A place for storing large amounts of products. In logistics, a place where products go to from the manufacturer before going to the retailer.
To store in a warehouse or similar.
To confine (a person) to an institution for a long period.
To acquire and then shelve, simply to prevent competitors from acquiring it.
definitions.example: the warehousing of syndicated TV shows

definitions.definition: A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.
A traveling block used on a cable in skidding logs.
The best possible hand in lowball.
A motorbike.
(usually in compounds specifying a context) a slut; a promiscuous woman
To travel or exercise using a bicycle.
An untrustworthy, despicable, or disreputable person, especially one suspected of being a criminal.
definitions.example: They say all the lowlifes used to hang out at the docks and plot their despicable crimes, before being elected to public office.

definitions.definition: To furnish with decorations.
definitions.example: We decorated the Christmas tree with tinsel and baubles.

definitions.definition: To improve the appearance of an interior of, as a house, room, or office.
definitions.example: There's some paint left over from when we decorated the guest bedroom.

definitions.definition: To decorate an interior space, as a house, room, or office.
definitions.example: People tend to decorate for the holidays or special events.

definitions.definition: To honor by providing a medal, ribbon, or other adornment.
definitions.example: He was a decorated soldier who served in three wars.

definitions.definition: To extend a method, etc. by attaching some further code item.
definitions.example: It makes sure that the field name argument is not empty, and that the field specified there is an actual existing field in the class which declares the method decorated with this attribute.
